title: function-typed val (Strategy)
manager: A function-typed val — behavior stored in the machine's extended state as a value you can set and swap — which is the Strategy pattern: the algorithm a machine uses becomes runtime-configurable data rather than hard-coded structure. Combined with the portable identity of functions, the chosen strategy serializes and replays with the rest of the state, so a machine's behavior travels with its snapshot.
cs: function-typed val (extended state); the Strategy pattern; swappable at runtime; serializes via the defunctionalization tag (W6.27).
